dependencies: Reliance on coal and natural gas suppliers for electric generation
Dependence on railroads and pipelines for fuel transportation
Reliance on MISO for wholesale electricity market operations
Dependence on contractors for construction and maintenance services
risks: Failure of suppliers to fulfill obligations to provide natural gas or coal
Counterparty financial or operational problems inhibiting fuel delivery
Supply chain disruptions and increased lead times
Barriers to accessing key materials for renewable projects (solar, battery, and other key equipment) created by geopolitical relations
Changes in commodity prices, applicable tariffs, and/or material and equipment availability
Inflation of labor rates and material and equipment prices

regulatory_risks: Changes in ROA (Retail Open Access) regulations allowing increased electric generation service from alternative suppliers
Creation of municipal utilities in Consumers' service territory or impairment of franchise rights
Adverse regulatory treatment or failure to receive timely regulatory orders from MPSC or FERC

Changes in federal or state laws or regulations related to energy policy, ROA, PURPA, infrastructure integrity or security, cybersecurity, gas pipeline safety, energy waste reduction, financial compensation mechanism, environment, reliability, health care reforms, taxes, accounting matters, climate change, air emissions, renewable energy, Dodd-Frank Act
MPSC rate regulation limiting recovery of costs or preventing rate increases
FERC regulation of wholesale electricity sales and natural gas pipeline rates
Environmental regulations under Clean Air Act, Clean Water Act, RCRA, CERCLA, Endangered Species Act, Migratory Bird Treaty Act
EPA regulations including MATS, CSAPR, NAAQS, Good Neighbor Plan, greenhouse gas emissions rules, CCR disposal rules, effluent limitation guidelines
Michigan 2023 Energy Law requirements for renewable energy (50% by 2030, 60% by 2035) and clean energy (80% by 2035, 100% by 2040)
U.S. Department of Transportation Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration rules for gas transmission pipelines and underground storage facilities
EGLE environmental permitting and compliance requirements
Potential regulation of greenhouse gases at federal, state, and local levels
Michigan Healthy Climate Plan goals for net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050
PFAS regulation at state and/or federal level requiring testing and remediation
Changes in taxation including federal, state, and local tax rates
Potential local clearing requirement for electric capacity in MISO Michigan footprint
